channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US Indicts Former Cuban Leader Raúl Castro Over 1996 Plane Shootdown
item.description: The US charged the 94-year-old former president with conspiracy to kill US nationals and murder over the downing of two Brothers to the Rescue aircraft that killed four people, including three Americans. Castro faces potential life imprisonment or death penalty if convicted.

item.title: STI Cases Hit Record Levels Across Europe
item.description: Gonorrhoea cases surged 303% since 2015 to over 106,000, while syphilis doubled to 45,557 cases in 2024. Health officials cite widening gaps in testing and prevention, with congenital syphilis in newborns nearly doubling from 2023.

item.title: Barney Frank, Gay Rights Trailblazer and Financial Reformer, Dies at 86
item.description: The longtime Democratic congressman became the first member of Congress to voluntarily come out as gay in 1987 and later the first incumbent lawmaker to marry someone of the same sex. He co-authored the 2010 Dodd-Frank financial reform law.

item.title: Pakistan PM to Visit China as Iran War Overshadows Talks
item.description: Islamabad and Beijing both seek to mediate in the Middle East conflict as Pakistani Prime Minister heads to China for discussions complicated by ongoing Iran war.

item.title: Austrian Spy Convicted of Passing Secrets to Wirecard Fugitive
item.description: Egisto Ott, former Austrian intelligence director, found guilty of passing classified information to Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek in major espionage case.

item.title: Iran Supreme Leader orders enriched uranium must stay in country
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei issued a directive that Iran's near-weapons-grade uranium cannot be sent abroad, hardening Tehran's stance and complicating US-Iran peace talks.

item.title: Iran rebuilding military industrial base faster than expected
item.description: Iran has restarted some drone production during the six-week ceasefire, with US intelligence assessments indicating faster-than-expected military industrial base reconstruction.

item.title: Pentagon official Beijing visit blocked over Taiwan arms package
item.description: China is holding up a proposed visit by Pentagon Under-Secretary Elbridge Colby as Beijing pressures Trump over a $14 billion weapons package for Taiwan.

item.title: UK firms suffer widespread activity slide from Iran war fallout
item.description: British companies are experiencing their most widespread drop in activity in over a year due to economic fallout from the Iran war and political uncertainty at home.

item.title: Investigation reveals unraveling of US diplomacy under Trump
item.description: Trump's threats, personal envoys and hollowed-out embassies are reshaping US diplomacy as allies from Europe to Asia forge new channels around personality-driven decisions.

item.title: US Preparing Indictment Against Former Cuban President Raúl Castro
item.description: The Justice Department is preparing to seek an indictment against former Cuban President Raúl Castro over the 1996 shootdown of two planes operated by Miami-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue, as US-Cuba tensions escalate following the Venezuela intervention.

item.title: Venezuela Deports Maduro Ally Alex Saab to Face US Prosecution
item.description: Venezuela deported Alex Saab, a close ally of ousted leader Nicolás Maduro who was previously pardoned by Biden in a prisoner swap, marking a major reversal as the new government cooperates with US investigations into the former regime.

item.title: Xi and Putin Strengthen Ties in Beijing Days After Trump Visit
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin oversaw signing of over 40 cooperation agreements, emphasizing growing energy trade and strategic alignment in their first meeting since Trump’s diplomatic visit to Beijing.

item.title: Iran Consolidates Control Over Hormuz Strait With Island Checkpoints
item.description: Reuters investigation reveals Iran has established a multi-tiered clearance system for vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, combining diplomatic deals, island checkpoints, and sometimes fees, as it consolidates control over the critical waterway.

item.title: Syria invited to G7 summit in France as guest nation
item.description: Syria will attend the G7 summit in France next month for the first time, with President Ahmed al-Sharaa representing the country in a major diplomatic breakthrough following years of isolation.

item.title: Merz proposes radical EU membership shakeup for Ukraine, Moldova
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz says the EU enlargement process takes too long and creates frustration, proposing a new framework to accelerate membership for Ukraine, Moldova, and Western Balkans.

item.title: Wes Streeting pledges wealth tax in Labour leadership challenge
item.description: UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting pledges to introduce a wealth tax if elected Labour leader, directly challenging PM Keir Starmer as pressure mounts on the prime minister.

item.title: Israeli Minister Condemned for Taunting Detained Gaza Flotilla Activists
item.description: Far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir posted a video taunting handcuffed pro-Palestinian activists from a Gaza-bound aid flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces, drawing criticism from France, Italy, and even Prime Minister Netanyahu.

item.title: Austrian Ex-Intelligence Officer Convicted of Spying for Russia
item.description: Egisto Ott, a former Austrian intelligence official, was sentenced to four years for passing secret information to Russian intelligence and fugitive Wirecard executive Jan Marsalek. The case has revived concerns about Austria as a hub for Russian espionage.

item.title: US Surveillance Flights Track Near Cuba Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Flight-tracking data shows US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and MQ-4C Triton drones operating within 50 miles of Cuba as Washington maintains pressure on the islands communist government following an effective oil blockade.

item.title: Sierra Leone Receives US Deportees Under Trump Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Nine West African migrants were flown to Sierra Leone as part of Donald Trumps mass deportation policy. The country agreed to accept up to 300 deportees annually, joining other African nations receiving expelled migrants.

item.title: US Threatens to Revoke Palestinian UN Delegation Visas
item.description: A State Department cable instructed US diplomats to pressure Palestinian officials to withdraw Ambassador Riyad Mansour candidacy for UN General Assembly vice president or face visa revocation. The Trump administration previously barred Palestinian officials from attending the UN General Assembly last year.

item.title: China-Linked Vessel Suspected in Taiwan Submarine Cable Cut
item.description: A submarine cable linking Matsu Island to Taiwan was severed in April, the third incident in four years. Analysis points to a Chinese dredger vessel with maritime militia ties operating under the pretense of a salvage operation. Taiwan faces infrastructure vulnerability as Chinese gray-zone tactics intensify.

item.title: Lithuanian Lawmakers Shelter Underground as Drone Violates Airspace
item.description: A drone violated Lithuanian airspace, forcing lawmakers to shelter underground and temporarily suspending flights at Vilnius airport. The incident is the latest in a series of security concerns in the Baltic region.

item.title: Global Oil Market Faces Supply Crunch Within Months Without Peace Deal
item.description: The oil industry has shown resilience during the Iran conflict, but analysts warn the global market may be only months away from a breaking point if peace talks fail to produce a breakthrough.
